title: Bruins assistant GM leaving organization to ‘pursue other opportunities’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jamie Langenbrunner is leaving his role as assistant GM with the Boston Bruins after 11 years with the team.
Langenbrunner was involved in player personnel and identification in his assistant GM role since 2022-23.
Potential replacements for Langenbrunner include Adam McQuaid and Zdeno Chara within the Bruins organization.

title: What Sam Kennedy said of Jason Varitek’s future with the Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jason Varitek remains with the Boston Red Sox but is reassigned after Alex Cora's dismissal.
Red Sox CEO Sam Kennedy states Varitek's future role is undecided, but a longstanding connection remains.
Reports suggest Varitek may not stay with the Red Sox, despite Kennedy's hopes of a long-term tenure.

title: Former Celtics star Isaiah Thomas hired by Boston as a pro, college scout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics have hired former star Isaiah Thomas, 37, as a pro and college scout based in Seattle.
Thomas, who left a lasting impact during his 2015-17 Celtics tenure, has joined the team's basketball operations staff.
Thomas participated in the NBA Combine in Chicago, aiding in prospect interviews and learning talent evaluation processes.

title: This play from Drake Maye’s rookie season continues to stand out to new Patriots safety
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye impressed new Patriots safety Mike Brown during a standout rookie play in a 2024 game against the Titans.
Maye's scrambling pass to Rhamondre Stevenson forced overtime, despite the Patriots' eventual 20-17 loss to the Titans.
New teammates, including tight end Julian Hill, praise Maye's leadership and influence during the Patriots' offseason workouts.

title: Worcester man goes viral for performance protesting potholes at city council meeting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Wilson Lam's performance at a Worcester City Council meeting protesting potholes gained viral attention, receiving over 402,200 views on TikTok.
The 24-year-old Worcester resident used sound effects and silent protest to highlight road issues lasting more than two decades.
Lam plans to address additional community issues at council meetings and has proposed a 2027 mayoral run with a focus on infrastructure.

title: Jared Wilson’s switch back to center is a ‘full-circle moment’ with Drake Maye
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jared Wilson transitions back to center for the New England Patriots in May 2026.
Wilson played left guard as a rookie but was drafted as a center from the University of Georgia.
The Patriots traded Garrett Bradbury, making room for Wilson to start as center alongside quarterback Drake Maye.

title: Karen Read’s Lexus SUV sold in private deal after low turnout canceled auction
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Auction of Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570 in Woburn was canceled due to low turnout.
The Lexus SUV, with about 12,000 miles, was sold in a private deal following auction cancellation.
Read was acquitted of charges related to a 2022 incident but faces a wrongful death lawsuit.

title: Bruins re-sign Lukas Reichel to a 1-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed restricted free agent Lukas Reichel to a 1-year contract with a $950,000 NHL cap hit.
Reichel, 23, played 10 games with Boston last season, scoring one goal and posting three points.
Acquired from Vancouver in March, Reichel will need to clear waivers if he doesn't make Boston's roster.

title: Boston city councilor criticizes ‘soft on crime’ progressives after Cambridge shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Councilor Ed Flynn criticized progressives for being 'soft on crime' following a Cambridge shooting incident.
Tyler Brown, accused of the shooting, had a history of violent offenses and was on probation for a 2020 incident.
Flynn argues the justice system favors criminals, citing Brown's case and calls for accountability in handling crime.

title: ‘Quality of life is plummeting’: Readers share their economic concerns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A MassINC poll indicates 42% of Massachusetts residents feel financially worse off compared to last year.
High taxes, unaffordable housing, and increasing healthcare costs are major financial challenges for Boston.com readers.
Massachusetts has the highest threshold for middle-class income, ranging between $69,885 and $209,656.
